market_summary: U.S. markets closed mixed-to-positive on May 22 (S&P 500 +0.37%, Nasdaq +0.19%), with the AI sector digesting NVIDIA's blowout Q1 earnings while broader tech names traded quietly ahead of the weekend. The AI infrastructure spending narrative remains firmly intact, with analysts raising price targets across the board following NVDA's results.

MSFT.headline: Veteran exec Yusuf Mehdi confirmed departing after fiscal year-end, while Anthropic is in talks to rent Microsoft-designed AI server chips — a positive signal for MSFT's custom silicon strategy.
MSFT.reasoning: Microsoft has been flagged as a relative laggard among hyperscalers, with analysts noting it has missed estimates for three consecutive quarters and that its heavy OpenAI dependency has created headwinds. However, the emerging Anthropic chip deal and a fresh $1B+ EY enterprise AI alliance suggest the AI monetization pipeline is broadening. RBC Capital reaffirmed a Buy rating this week; next earnings are July 28, 2026 — a key catalyst to watch for cloud re-acceleration.

AMZN.headline: Amazon secured a legal victory defeating a tariff-evasion appeal, while AWS Trainium AI chip demand surged — Wells Fargo maintained Overweight with a $312 price target.
AMZN.reasoning: Amazon's AWS cloud revenue growth accelerated to +28% in Q1 2026, following +24% and +20% in the prior two quarters — a clear re-acceleration trend that underpins the long-term thesis. The Trainium chip demand surge reduces AWS dependency on third-party silicon and improves margins structurally. Jeff Bezos publicly stated AI fears are overblown, reinforcing management's conviction in the AI build-out. No action warranted; hold with confidence.

NVDA.headline: NVIDIA reported fiscal Q1 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY), beat guidance of $78B, and guided Q2 to $91B (+95% YoY) — a post-earnings 'sell the news' dip followed a historic print.
NVDA.reasoning: The post-earnings pullback of ~1.9% is classic 'sell the news' behavior after a 62%+ run over the past year; the underlying fundamentals are exceptional. Q2 guidance of $91B crushed the $87.2B consensus, and NVIDIA's all-time high was just set on May 14 at $236.54. The CEO did acknowledge the China AI chip market is largely ceded to Huawei — a known headwind — but domestic and global hyperscaler demand more than compensates. Long-term holders should sit tight.

GOOGL.headline: Alphabet avoided a Chrome breakup in the DOJ antitrust case and is now seeking to overturn the broader search monopoly ruling, while Q1 results showed sales +22% and Google Cloud revenue +63% YoY.
GOOGL.reasoning: Alphabet's Q1 2026 results were exceptionally strong — $110B in revenue with Google Cloud surging to $20B — and operating margins expanded 220 basis points year over year. The antitrust overhang is easing with the Chrome divestiture off the table, and the stock is up 124% over the past year. At a P/E of ~29x with Cloud growing at 63%, valuation remains reasonable for a long-term compounder; next earnings July 22, 2026.

alerts.message: Monitor Q4 FY2026 earnings on July 28 closely — third consecutive quarter of missing estimates could pressure the stock; watch for Azure re-acceleration signals and any update on the OpenAI partnership economics.

long_term_view: All four holdings remain core AI infrastructure beneficiaries with durable competitive moats — NVDA in accelerated compute, AMZN/GOOGL in cloud re-acceleration, and MSFT in enterprise AI adoption. The only near-term concern is MSFT's relative underperformance versus peers, but its resources and diversified AI strategy make a long-term thesis change premature.
